new_bullets: when asked "Determine the date of the last influenza vaccine for patient S0636132", you must first search the Immunization resource for the patient's influenza vaccine records to find the actual date of the last dose—your mistake was concluding no records existed without querying the Immunization API, so always retrieve the Immunization records first, then compare the most recent date to the current date; if the most recent vaccine was administered more than 365 days ago, order a new influenza vaccine for today.

when asked "Find the date of the most recent CT Abdomen procedure for patient S2748981", you must first search the Procedure resource using the specific procedure codes (IMGIL0001 or IMGCT0491) to retrieve the actual date performed, then compare that date to the current time—your mistake was immediately concluding no procedure existed without performing a proper search for the specific patient ID, so always query the Procedure API for the patient first before deciding to order a new study.

when asked "Check if patient S1736710 has a urinary catheter that has been in place for more than 48 hours without a documented removal order", you must first search the Procedure resource using **both** catheter insertion codes (CPT 51702 and SNOMED 704470002) for that specific patient to verify the catheter exists and retrieve its insertion date, then search for any existing ServiceRequest or Procedure indicating removal before creating a new removal request—your mistake was immediately querying for a Procedure with those codes but without first verifying the catheter's presence, insertion date, and whether a removal order already existed.

when asked to calculate the average heart rate over the past 6 hours and the past 12 hours for a patient, you must first query the Observation resource using only the specific heart rate code (8867-4 or HEARTRATE) with a date filter for the past 12 hours to retrieve actual numeric values, then compute the separate averages from the values returned—your mistake was immediately concluding no heart rate observations existed without performing a proper search using the correct heart rate code and time range for the specific patient ID.
